headline: Rehabilitation Drug Programs Show 78% Relapse Rate Within 2 Years
context: Government drug rehabilitation centers report high relapse rates indicating need for approach reform.

cards.big: 78% of drug rehabilitation center graduates relapse within 2 years of release
cards.sub: RM480 million spent annually on institutional rehabilitation with poor outcomes

cards.big: AADK operates 22 mandatory detention centers holding 6,800 residents as of 2025
cards.sub: Compulsory residential model restricts liberty without evidence of treatment effectiveness

cards.big: Post-release community support is available for only 15% of rehabilitation graduates
cards.sub: Absence of aftercare and reintegration services drives the high relapse rate

cards.big: Portugal's community-based treatment model achieves 62% abstinence rate at 2-year follow-up
cards.sub: Voluntary treatment with housing and employment support outperforms compulsory detention

cards.big: Locking people up for addiction and releasing them without support is not rehabilitation—it is a cycle
Should Malaysia replace compulsory detention centers with voluntary community treatment programs
